comment.body:
+.92% from the close 6 months ago.  With a pretty much 120 point ⬆️ move on spy since April without a pullback and a 92% move the past 5 years.

6 months ago we were worried about 2 countries blowing each other up.  Today we are worried about the world blowing up, critical resources being shut off, and nuclear warfare AND we are still up .92% from 6 months ago

Wake up…We are fucked

comment.body: I’m with you, I’m more cynical than most on the macro views and unsustainable nature of the status quo.  The artificial buoyancy of the markets is driven by liquidity expansion (either monetary or fiscal), supply side economics benefits asset holders visa vie the stock market.  Despite Trump’s initial hawkish signaling he’s using the same play book we’ve used post 2008, increase fiscal spending and ease monetary policy with lower rates or QE

comment.body:
Hi Alma,
I am looking forward by your post today.

After the move in the second half of the day and the VIX barely reacting, I was still leaning neutral to bearish. But overnight, the /VX painted a different picture. That looks like capitulation to me. I believe the VIX will catch up and confirm this shift soon. It feels like we’re seeing a regime change.

In hindsight, this wasn’t really about Iran at all, it was about oil. When oil spiked, markets pulled back. When oil fell, equities rebounded. It’s clear now that the real fear was inflation risk driven by oil prices.
